name: Setup Mailjet Email Service
description: End-to-end Mailjet campaign email service setup. Reads all configuration from a mailjet-service-config DataQube, validates credentials, embeds a logo image, creates responsive HTML+text templates from a copy document, sends test emails, and writes all template IDs back to the DataQube. Idempotent — safe to re-run to update existing templates.

requiredDataQube.description: Provides all inputs (credentials, sender identity, branding, campaign config) before the skill runs. Template IDs are written back to populated_values on completion.

MAILJET_API_KEY.description: Mailjet public API key — read from DataQube credentials section

MAILJET_SECRET_KEY.description: Mailjet secret API key — read from DataQube credentials section

MAILJET_FROM_NAME.description: Sender display name shown to recipients

MAILJET_FROM_EMAIL.description: Verified sender email address — must be verified in Mailjet account

MAILJET_LOGO_PATH.description: Relative path to logo image (PNG or JPG). Embedded as base64 — no external hosting required.

MAILJET_CAMPAIGN_ID.description: Unique campaign identifier used to namespace template names (e.g. ks_prospects)

MAILJET_COPY_DOC.description: Path to campaign copy markdown doc containing email sequence definitions

MAILJET_TEST_EMAIL.description: If provided, sends a test of Email 1 to this address after template creation

MAILJET_EMAIL_NUMBER.description: If provided, creates/updates only this email number (1-based). Omit to process all.

MAILJET_UPDATE.description: If true, force-updates content of templates that already exist

template_ids.description: Map of env var name → Mailjet template ID for all created templates. Also written back to DataQube populated_values.

amplify_env_block.description: Ready-to-paste block of MAILJET_TEMPLATE_* env vars for Amplify

templates_created.description: Number of new templates created in this run

templates_updated.description: Number of existing templates updated in this run

capabilities.name: Load Config from DataQube
capabilities.description: Reads all non-secret inputs from the mailjet-service-config DataQube populated_values. Secrets (MAILJET_API_KEY, MAILJET_SECRET_KEY) are loaded from .env.local only.

capabilities.name: Validate Mailjet Credentials
capabilities.description: Verifies MAILJET_API_KEY and MAILJET_SECRET_KEY by calling GET /v3/REST/template

capabilities.name: Embed Logo Image
capabilities.description: Reads logo from MAILJET_LOGO_PATH, resizes to max 560px wide via Pillow, encodes as base64 data URI. Falls back to text wordmark if no logo found.

capabilities.name: Create Email Templates
capabilities.description: Creates responsive HTML + plain-text Mailjet templates from campaign copy doc. Header: white background with logo. Body: dark (#111111) with gold (#f5c842) CTA button. Footer: sender attribution + unsubscribe link.

capabilities.name: Send Test Email
capabilities.description: Sends a test of a specified email (default: Email 1) to MAILJET_TEST_EMAIL using the created template, with live KS URL and first_name variable populated.

capabilities.name: Persist Template IDs to DataQube
capabilities.description: Writes all MAILJET_TEMPLATE_* IDs back to the mailjet-service-config DataQube populated_values section, so they are available to send scripts and future skill runs without manual env var setup.

capabilities.name: Output Env Var Block
capabilities.description: Prints all MAILJET_TEMPLATE_* env var names and IDs ready to paste into Amplify or .env.local

steps.name: load_config
steps.prompt: Read populated_values from data/iqubes/mailjet-service-config.dataqube.json; overlay with .env.local for secrets

steps.name: validate_creds
steps.prompt: GET /v3/REST/template to verify credentials and fetch existing template names

steps.name: embed_logo
steps.prompt: Read logo file, resize with Pillow (560px max), encode as base64 data URI

steps.name: parse_copy_doc
steps.prompt: Parse campaign copy markdown doc to extract subject, preheader, body, and CTA per email

steps.name: create_templates
steps.prompt: POST /v3/REST/template for each email in sequence (skip if exists and not --update)

steps.name: set_content
steps.prompt: POST /v3/REST/template/{id}/detailcontent with HTML shell + parsed copy per email

steps.name: write_back_ids
steps.prompt: Update populated_values in data/iqubes/mailjet-service-config.dataqube.json with all MAILJET_TEMPLATE_* IDs returned by Mailjet

steps.name: send_test
steps.prompt: POST /v3.1/send test email if MAILJET_TEST_EMAIL provided

steps.name: output_ids
steps.prompt: Print MAILJET_TEMPLATE_{CAMPAIGN}_{N} env var block ready for Amplify

requirements: Pillow (pip install Pillow) — optional, used for logo resizing
metadata.notes: From email must be a verified sender in the Mailjet account before templates can be used for live sends.
